def compute_factor(df: pl.DataFrame) -> pl.Series:
    # Sort by (code, date) for per-code rolling computations
    df = df.sort(['code', 'date'])

    # Rolling means of close
    mean_8 = rolling_mean(pl.col('close'), window=8)
    mean_2 = rolling_mean(pl.col('close'), window=2)

    # Rolling stddev of close (8-day)
    std_8 = rolling_std(pl.col('close'), window=8)

    # 20-day average volume (adv20)
    adv20 = rolling_mean(pl.col('volume'), window=20)

    # Condition 1: (mean_8 + std_8) < mean_2  -> -1
    cond1 = (mean_8 + std_8) < mean_2

    # Condition 2: mean_2 < (mean_8 - std_8)   -> 1
    cond2 = mean_2 < (mean_8 - std_8)

    # Condition 3: 1 < (volume/adv20) OR (volume/adv20) == 1  -> 1, else -1
    # Equivalent to (volume / adv20) >= 1
    cond3 = (pl.col('volume') / adv20) >= 1

    # Nested ternary: cond1 ? -1 : (cond2 ? 1 : (cond3 ? 1 : -1))
    result = pl.when(cond1).then(-1).otherwise(
        pl.when(cond2).then(1).otherwise(
            pl.when(cond3).then(1).otherwise(-1)
        )
    )

    return df.select(result).to_series()
